dialogue.utterance: Unit 2, dispatch for breathing problems at 123 Redacted in [CITY].
Hi ma'am, I'm Nicole with the rescue squad. What brought you to call 911 today?
I am having a hard time breathing. It started yesterday and has gotten worse today.
Ma'am, can you give me your name and the date?
My name is Evelyn and I don't know what today's date is.
That's okay. What city are we in?
We're in [CITY].
Ma'am, can you point to your right hand? Thank you.
Ma'am, can you please open your eyes for me?
Of course.
What were you doing when you first realized things were getting worse?
I was walking in my bedroom when I noticed I could not catch my breath. I used my inhaler, but nothing helped.
Did the situation go from okay to bad in minutes or slowly over the day?
It started getting worse an hour ago. Like it just increased all at once.
Ma'am, can you speak in complete sentences right now?
No, I have trouble even getting one word out without thinking I'm going to pass out.
Ma'am, can you please take a deep breath in and out for me?
Her chest seems to rise and fall symmetrically, but she's breathing very rapidly and wheezing is audible.
Partner, let's get the pulse oximeter attached.
Oxygen saturation is 92%.
Her respiratory rate is 39 breaths per minute and labored.
Pulse is 93 and regular.
Blood pressure is 221 over 97 mmHg, very elevated systolic number.
Evelyn, I'm going to check your mental status with some basic questions.
Do you know what year it is?
I think it's 2019.
Great. What time is it approximately?
Just so we are clear, where are we right now?
[CITY], [STATE].
Evelyn, can I use a stethoscope now to listen to your breath sounds?
Evelyn, can I press gently over your belly for a bit while feeling for tenderness?
Please go ahead.
No pain or rigidity in the abdomen, and no signs of gastrointestinal distention.
Partner, check the extremities for angulation or deformity as we move onto the next part.
No deformities noted in extremities and spine.
Evelyn, can you move your arms and legs for me? Let's start with your arms going up and then your legs?
Sure, I can do that.
Monitor is showing sinus rhythm with a regular heart rate, no evidence of arrhythmia right now.
Let's get a 4-lead ECG tracing now for baseline.
Now we'll complete a 12-lead ECG while she is still stable. All clear to proceed.
Partner, place all electrodes as needed to ensure proper capture of lead information.
EKG is ready for interpretation: abnormal Q in inferior leads, RBBB, and underlying left anterior fascicular block noted. Please review for transmission.
Transport team, EKG reveals signs of 2nd degree AV block, Mobitz type II with occasional ventricular premature complexes. Keep this in mind when reviewing upon arrival at [COMPANY_NAME].
I will administer Albuterol and Atrovent via nebulizer. Please stay calm and try to breathe in deeply with each breath into the mask.
Albuterol nebulizer administered: 5 mg total, per standing order protocol at this time.
Ipratropium nebulizer given: 0.5 mg administered per standing order now as well to help open up her airways further.
Her breath sounds significantly improved post treatment. She can now speak complete sentences with minimal difficulty after being on the nebulizer treatment.
Talking 10 minutes later, respiratory rate is down to 30 breaths per minute now which is a positive sign of improvement.
Pulse remains at 92. Oxygen saturation has come up to 100% from 92% initially. Great improvement seen here.
Blood pressure reduced to 186 over 133 mmHg post-treatment. Still elevated but indicating some stabilization of her condition overall.
Evelyn, have you had any chest pain or pressure since we started our treatment this morning prior to medication administration?
No, just the difficulty breathing earlier was the main issue I had up until about an hour ago when it suddenly worsened significantly before help came out.
Evelyn, how do you feel now if I may ask directly about your current state of being after receiving care here on site now?.
Much better than earlier. I can now speak without gasping for air or trying to catch my breath mid-sentence these days.
She seems alert and oriented, with improved breathing and no signs of distress now considering previous issue of labored respiration yesterday lasting into today)));
Performing blood glucose check now with fingerstick and portable glucometer.
Reading is zero units per volume. No current symptoms of hypoglycemia suspected at this time, normal for diabetic patients or non.
Evelyn, we will transport you to [COMPANY_NAME] for further evaluation. Is there any other facility you prefer instead of [COMPANY_NAME] as final destination ?.
[COMPANY_NAME] is fine with me. I trust the care they provide very well through experience.
Partner, let's go ahead and safely position Evelyn onto our stretcher for transport now as discussed previously during briefing.
Taking roll with stretcher now. Let's move with the patient secured and in a comfortable posture relevant to any breathing needs.
Per general protocol 2-7, we are managing this as a potential cardiac arrest scenario based on initial findings observed through patient presentation.
All set. Wheeling towards the ambulance, where we will enter on code status green as standard procedure requires for transport.
Transport started, vital signs remain stable at this point with pulse 92, respiratory rate 30, and oxygens at 100% saturation now following interventions.
Evelyn, do your nostrils flare when you breathe in and when you exhale ?.
Yes, they did. Still easing up now post nebulizer administration as part of care plan today.
Noting her skin is warm and pink at this time subsequent to initial exam done earlier while still at residence before transport began.
Breath sounds are now clear. No wheezing or crackles detected with repeat stethoscope use recently conducted.
Her abdomen is flat and soft with no masses palpated during exam earlier on at her home today.
Respiratory rate has improved from initial 39 down to 30 breaths per minute now. Depth seems less labored but still rapid as seen upon reassessment post-neb tx
To make breathing easier, was there a specific position you found helpful earlier before beginning treatment with nebulizers ?.
I was sitting upright most of the time, tried lying down but that made it worse overall prior to arrival today.
Evelyn, do you know the name of the current U.S. President other than knowing that it's 2019 currently according to earlier answers you gave today ?.
I believe it's Donald Trump and he is from the red party based on prior knowledge retained from TV watching sessions last four years.
Comparing radial pulse to central pulses, all pulses are strong and synchronous indicating healthy perfusion status now at bedside.
Evelyn, can you stay on this nebulizer for a little while longer just to reinforce the effects of bronchodilation initiated here on scene now ?.
Of course, as long as it helps me breathe better right now than previously experienced throughout this episode yesterday into today。
Let's monitor her over the next few minutes and see if she continues to improve with this treatment approach currently employed.
No signs of wounds or abrasions on the legs or hands. Clean, dry skin observed at this time indicating good hygiene practices at home today.
Evelyn, we will be entering the ambulance shortly. Please signal if you need support during the transfer from stretcher to interior seating on board now ?.
Preparing to stretch up to ramp of the ambulance now. Please remain calm and allow us to guide you safely onboard as we ensure medical safety throughout ride.
You're all set Evelyn, will now fasten the straps and adjust you for safe transport with comfort considered as primary goal in moving forward with potential transportation needs.
